blockingIssues.description: VllmBackend::adopt() verifies only that some compose-managed container is running, then stores the caller-provided container_id without verifying that specific ID is alive.
VllmBackend::stop() ignores docker compose down errors and still clears internal state/returns Ok, allowing false Stopped state when container teardown fails.
ModelInfoResponse uses skip_serializing_if on props, so vLLM /props 404 paths omit the props key instead of returning props: null as required.
VllmBackend::start() calls Docker availability checks before compose generation; this violates the requirement that compose-generation failures return before any Docker command executes.
All env-gated integration tests share fixed host port 19876 while using parallelizable tokio tests, causing nondeterministic port-binding conflicts when ROOKERY_INTEGRATION=1.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add integration-test isolation guidance: env-gated Docker tests must avoid shared fixed ports/resources across parallel tests (use unique ports, unique compose project names, or serialized execution).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature vllm-integration-tests uses a shared fixed port across multiple #[tokio::test] cases, creating a race-prone design under parallel test execution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether rust-backend fail-first TDD is mandatory or aspirational; if mandatory, require explicit red-phase evidence in worker verification sections.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature vllm-compose-generation transcript showed implementation plus tests added together without an explicit red-phase run, despite current skill wording requiring fail-first TDD.
